title: zornia albiflora
intro: Zornia albiflora, also known as White Zornia or White Wild Galuga, is a tropical plant native to Central and South America, belonging to the Legume family. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, including relief from fever, colds, and respiratory issues, as well as acting as a natural remedy for wounds and skin infections. Zornia albiflora is often prepared as a tea, decoction, or infusional remedy, and its leaves and flowers are used in herbal preparations, typically consumed or applied topically to experience its medicinal effects.
